faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my East New York home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in woolen clothing, silky webbing in closet corners, small cream-colored larvae on fabrics, and adult moths flying around wardrobes. You may also notice damage to carpets, upholstery, or other natural fiber materials. Our technicians provide free inspections to confirm the presence of clothes moths and identify the species affecting your home.

faqs.question: What causes the price range of $550 to $5,000 for clothes moth control?
faqs.answer: The cost depends on the severity of infestation, size of the affected area, and treatment method required. A single-room treatment with minor infestation typically costs $550-$900, while whole-home treatments for severe infestations in larger East New York residences can reach $5,000. Multi-unit buildings may require additional treatments. We provide detailed quotes after our initial inspection.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take in East New York?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on your home's size and infestation severity. Most infestations require 2-3 follow-up visits over 4-8 weeks to target newly hatched larvae and ensure complete elimination. We offer same-day emergency service for East New York residents and flexible scheduling to accommodate your needs.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for families and pets when applied by our licensed technicians. We prioritize eco-friendly solutions and integrated pest management techniques. After treatment, we recommend staying out of treated areas for 2-4 hours while products dry. All our methods are specifically designed to target moths while protecting your household's health and your valuable fabrics.
